name: superpowers
description: Agentic skills framework for coding agents -- anti-rationalization patterns, HARD-GATE enforcement, two-stage review, TDD discipline, subagent orchestration with model selection, Graphviz dot process flows

cherry_picks.description: Rationalization tables and red-flag lists that preempt LLM excuse-making. Applied to LAW 4/5.
XML-like <HARD-GATE> tags in skill definitions that block progression without user approval.
Enforced ordering: spec compliance review before code quality review. Prevents polishing wrong code.
Graphviz dot notation embedded in skill definitions for dual-purpose docs + agent-parseable process flows.
Three-tier model selection (cheap/standard/capable) with complexity signals for agent dispatch.

notes: Cherry-pick ideas only -- do NOT absorb their anti-MCP stance, mandatory-TDD absolutism, or lack of personas. Focus on: (1) anti-rationalization tables for LAW 4/5, (2) <HARD-GATE> markers for skills requiring user approval, (3) two-stage review ordering (spec compliance then code quality), (4) Graphviz dot diagrams in skill definitions, (5) subagent model selection guidance. Their skills are MIT-licensed markdown SKILL.md files -- same format as PAI skills, low friction to adapt.
